seo_title: Solid Bronze Jewelry: Wear & Care Guide | Moon Raven Designs
meta_description: Solid bronze jewelry from Moon Raven Designs: an earthy, durable metal with a warm glow. Learn about its properties, patina, care, and who chooses it.
intro: Solid bronze is an alloy of copper and tin, with a warm, golden-brown hue that deepens over time. It has been used for millennia—from ancient tools to modern sculpture—for its strength and resonant beauty. At Moon Raven Designs, we work in solid bronze because it holds fine detail and develops a unique, living patina that tells the story of wear.
properties: Solid bronze is dense and substantial, with a satisfying weight that feels grounded and enduring. Our bronze is typically a tin bronze alloy (approximately 88% copper, 12% tin), which gives it a rich, warm color and excellent corrosion resistance. Unlike brass, bronze has a redder, more earthy tone. It will tarnish gradually, developing a brownish-green patina that many find beautiful. Bronze is not hypoallergenic—the copper content can react with some skin, causing a greenish stain that is harmless and washes off. It is very durable and suitable for everyday wear, though it can scratch over time. Rings may need resizing less often than silver, as bronze is harder.
care: To clean solid bronze, use a soft cloth and mild soap with water; dry thoroughly. Avoid chlorine, salt water, and harsh household cleaners, which can accelerate tarnish or cause pitting. To refresh the patina, you can use a commercial bronze cleaner or a paste of lemon juice and baking soda—but many prefer the natural aged look. Store in a dry place, away from humidity. For deep scratches or professional refinishing, bring your piece to a jeweler. Avoid wearing in the shower or while swimming to prolong the finish.
who_chooses: Solid bronze is chosen by those who appreciate earthy, understated elegance and a metal that ages with character. It suits statement rings, sculptural pendants, and cuffs—pieces meant to be worn daily and become part of the wearer. It carries an intent of connection to history and the natural world, often favored by artisans, writers, and anyone drawn to materials that live and change.

faq.q: Is solid bronze jewelry good quality?
faq.a: Yes, solid bronze is a high-quality, durable metal that holds fine detail and develops a beautiful patina over time. It is stronger than brass and more affordable than precious metals, making it an excellent choice for everyday wear.

faq.q: Does solid bronze turn skin green?
faq.a: It can. The copper content in bronze may react with your skin's acidity, leaving a harmless green stain that washes off easily. This is more likely with new jewelry or in hot, humid weather. A clear lacquer or wax coating can reduce this effect.

faq.q: Does solid bronze jewelry tarnish or rust?
faq.a: Bronze tarnishes gradually, forming a brownish-green patina that many find attractive. It does not rust (which requires iron), but it can corrode if exposed to harsh chemicals or salt water. Proper care minimizes unwanted tarnish.

faq.q: Can you wear solid bronze in the shower?
faq.a: It's best to avoid wearing bronze in the shower. Soap and water can accelerate tarnish, and moisture trapped against the skin may increase the chance of green staining. Remove bronze jewelry before showering, swimming, or exercising.

While bronze has low intrinsic metal value compared to silver or gold, handcrafted solid bronze jewelry holds value as an art object. Its worth lies in design, craftsmanship, and durability—not scrap metal price.

faq.q: How can you tell if jewelry is solid bronze?
faq.a: Look for a warm, reddish-brown color and a dense weight. Solid bronze is non-magnetic. A hallmark or stamp may say 'bronze' or 'solid bronze.' If unsure, a jeweler can test with a drop of nitric acid—bronze will react with a greenish fizz.
